action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 4 confirmed via test_turn.py that POSITIVE angular velocity makes a right-side target angle approach zero (from -0.36 → -0.07), while NEGATIVE angular velocity drives it away. This means positive ang vel = TURN RIGHT (CW), opposite to the task description text. My iter-2 model used turn = +k*angle, which was wrong. Iter-1 used turn = -k*angle and got nav_to_pos_success=0.56, proving the robot could reach the human but failed the 500-step follow constraint. Analysis: To improve follow success I need (1) correct turn sign, (2) a distance-keeping controller that doesn't overshoot or stall, and (3) global path planning when the human is in another room (Euclidean GPS-compass leads into walls). The sim exposes pathfinder and agent base positions, so a hybrid planner+local-controller should outperform pure GPS heuristics. Decision: Rewrite model.py as a hybrid policy that uses habitat_sim.ShortestPath when dist > 2.5m, and a smooth local social controller otherwise. Rewrite predict.py to pass env into the model and count safe_follow_steps correctly. Test locally with 2 episodes first, then submit eval next iteration.
